instruction:
You are an A-Level Chemistry examiner.
Topic: S-block and selected inorganic chemistry. Block: mixed_inorganic. Student level: mixed.

Notes:
Use A-Level inorganic chemistry knowledge and the exam style from the verified question bank. The questions may cover s-block, p-block, d-block, periodic trends, oxides, hydrides, acids, transition metal chemistry, qualitative analysis, solubility, thermal stability, redox behaviour, and molecular structure. Keep the output format strict and consistent.

Generate exactly 15 multiple-choice A-Level chemistry questions as a JSON array.
The 15 questions must include exactly 5 easy, 5 medium, and 5 hard questions, in any order.
Each object must have exactly these keys: question_text, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_answer (value: A, B, C, or D), difficulty (value: easy, medium, or hard).
Output raw JSON array only. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
output: [{"question_text": "Which of the following statement/s is/are true regarding Sulphur and its compounds? (a) Sulphur is present as a crown shaped molecule with eight membered ring that has a cyclic zigzag arrangement. (b) Sulphurous acid (H2SO3) has the permanent bleaching ability. (c) Since the strength of repulsion between bond pair electrons in H2S is less when compared to H2O, the bond angle of H2S is less. (d) Sulphur is oxidized as SO2 by hot concentrated HNO3.", "option_a": "a,c,d", "option_b": "a,d", "option_c": "all are correct", "option_d": "a only", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "The incorrect statement regarding third period elements and their respective compounds is,", "option_a": "The acidic nature of their highest oxidation state oxides increases along the period from left to right.", "option_b": "In the oxy acids formed by elements Si ,P, S and Cl, those elements are found in sp3 hybridization.", "option_c": "The ability to form covalent compounds by these elements increases along the period from left to right.", "option_d": "When considering their stable ions, Al and Si have the smallest and largest radii respectively.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement/s regarding the properties of 3d block elements is/are true? (a) All transition elements take various valencies and form coloured compounds. (b) Even though the electronegativity increases because of the increase in nuclear charge along the period, Mn and Zn deviate from this trend due to their stable electronic configuration. (c) 50Co isotope is used as a radioactive source in cancer treatments. (d) Their metallic bond strength depends only on their ionic radius and the number of electrons provided for bonding.", "option_a": "b only", "option_b": "a,d", "option_c": "b,c", "option_d": "a,c,d", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is true regarding the electrical conductivity of weak acid solutions?", "option_a": "When the temperature of solution is increased, the random motion of ions increases so the conductivity of solution decreases.", "option_b": "When diluted with water, the degree of ionization of weak acid increases so there is an increase in conductivity.", "option_c": "To a weak acid HA, when its sodium salt NaA is added, equilibrium shifts backwards due to the common ion effect of A and the conductivity of solution decreases.", "option_d": "Conductivity of weak acid solutions depends on the polarity of the solvent in which it is dissolved.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is incorrect regarding the S block elements,", "option_a": "They contain valence electrons only in S orbitals.", "option_b": "Ca, Sr and Ba cause an increase in the pH when they are mixed with both NaOH and HCl solutions", "option_c": "He belongs to S block", "option_d": "All react with water and release H2 gas at room temperature.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "The effective nuclear charge which is felt by the one valence electron of Magnesium (Mg) is ?", "option_a": "Greater than that of Sodium (Na)", "option_b": "Equal to +1", "option_c": "Equal to +12", "option_d": "Greater than that of Aluminium (Al)", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following is/are correct regarding chemistry of alkali metals, alkali earth metals? (a) Since the charge density of their cations decreases down the group, their polarizing power decreases in the same order. (b) Electron affinity values of alkali metals are negative and electron affinity values of alkali earth metals are positive. (c) All halide salts of them are water soluble. (d) At least two products are obtained when they're heated in the air.", "option_a": "b only", "option_b": "a,d", "option_c": "all are correct", "option_d": "a,c,d", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "The reaction which correctly indicates the standard lattice dissociation of CaCl2 is", "option_a": "Ca2+(g) +2Cl-(g) -> CaCl2(s)", "option_b": "CaCl2(aq)-> Ca2+(aq) + 2C1-(aq)", "option_c": "CaCl2(s)->Ca(s) +2Cl(g)", "option_d": "CaCl2(s)->Ca2+(g)+2Cl (g)", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Ground state electronic configurations of atoms of different elements are given below. The electronic configuration of the element which has the highest electron affinity is,", "option_a": "[Ne]3s²3p⁴", "option_b": "[Ne]3s²3p²", "option_c": "[Ne]3s²3p³", "option_d": "[He]2s²2p⁴", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Select the incorrect statement", "option_a": "Ne has the highest negative electron affinity among second period elements.", "option_b": "Cl shows the highest valency among third period elements", "option_c": "N forms the largest stable anion among second period elements", "option_d": "Na has the highest effective nuclear charge among third period elements", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following shows buffer action? (a) KHCO3 (b) Na2CO3 (c) Na2CO3/NaHCO3 (d) Na2HPO4 (e) KCN/KSCN", "option_a": "(a) only", "option_b": "(c), (d) only", "option_c": "(b), (e) only", "option_d": "(a), (c), (d) only", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct? (a) Basic strength of group 1A hydrides increases down the group. (b) Polarizing ability of group 1A cations decreases down the group (c) Electrical conductivity of pure water cannot be changed by adding a piece of Na", "option_a": "a,b", "option_b": "b,c", "option_c": "a,b,c", "option_d": "a only", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding some s-block elements and compounds?", "option_a": "The reactivity of group 1 and group 2 elements decreases while going down the group.", "option_b": "Only Na, K, Rb, Cs and Ba are capable of forming peroxides among s-block elements.", "option_c": "The hydrogen carbonates of group 2 elements are stable only in aqueous state.", "option_d": "Group 1 ionic solids are generally soluble in water due to the positive nature of Gibb's free energy change of their dissolution process.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "The increasing order of the first ionization energy of the atoms Li, Be, B, Na and Si,", "option_a": "Na < Li < Si <B<Be", "option_b": "Na < Li < B <Si <Be", "option_c": "Na < Si < Li<B<Be", "option_d": "Na < Li < B <Be <Si", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement is incorrect?", "option_a": "Solubility of alkali earth metal oxides increases down the group.", "option_b": "When K reacts with excess oxygen, KO2 is obtained as the main product", "option_c": "The thermal stability of alkali earth metal bicarbonates is lesser than corresponding alkali metal bicarbonates as the covalent character of alkali earth metal bicarbonates is higher than that of respective alkali metal bicarbonates.", "option_d": "All the s-block metal hydrides are ionic compounds.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}]

instruction:
You are an A-Level Chemistry examiner.
Topic: A-Level inorganic chemistry mixed topics. Block: mixed_inorganic. Student level: mixed.

Notes:
Use A-Level inorganic chemistry knowledge and the exam style from the verified question bank. The questions may cover s-block, p-block, d-block, periodic trends, oxides, hydrides, acids, transition metal chemistry, qualitative analysis, solubility, thermal stability, redox behaviour, and molecular structure. Keep the output format strict and consistent.

Generate exactly 15 multiple-choice A-Level chemistry questions as a JSON array.
The 15 questions must include exactly 5 easy, 5 medium, and 5 hard questions, in any order.
Each object must have exactly these keys: question_text, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_answer (value: A, B, C, or D), difficulty (value: easy, medium, or hard).
Output raw JSON array only. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
output: [{"question_text": "Which one of the following statements regarding some elements/ compounds in S block and P- block is false?", "option_a": "𝐶𝑠 has the highest melting point among the elements of group 1", "option_b": "Except 𝐵𝑒𝐶𝑂3, all the other group 2 carbonates do not dissolve in water.", "option_c": "Due to its net-work covalent structure, B has the highest melting point among the elements which belong to its group.", "option_d": "In the super oxide of 𝐾, (𝐾𝑂2) oxygen is in the oxidation states of -1 and 0.", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Correct statement out of the following is,", "option_a": "Bond energy of SF2, SF4 and SF6 increases in the order of SF2 < SF4< SF6", "option_b": "pH of salt solutions of NaClO, NaClO2 and NaClO3 having equal concentration increases in the order of NaClO3 < NaClO2 < NaClO", "option_c": "Dipole – dipole interaction plays the major role in the variation of boiling point among HCl, HBrand HI", "option_d": "Ability of involving to hydrolysis among MgCl2, CaCl2 and SrCl2 varies in the order of MgCl2< CaCl2< SrCl2", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is correct regarding CH4, NH3 and H2O which are hydrides of second period non-metallic elements?", "option_a": "Tetrahedral, trigonal pyramidal and angular shape are the respective electron pair geometries of them.", "option_b": "Strength of intermolecular attractive forces between the molecules increase in the order of CH4, NH3, H2O", "option_c": "The electron repulsing strength between the bond pairs vary as H2O < NH3 < CH4", "option_d": "They are the hydrides with the highest boiling point in their respective groups.", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Identify the true statement from the followings?", "option_a": "N2 and NH4Cl are obtained in the reaction of NH3 gas with excess Cl2 gas.", "option_b": "Nitrous acid involves in disproportionation reaction to give nitric acid and colourless nitrous oxide.", "option_c": "In an aqueous solution, the decomposition of H2O2 is induced by acids.", "option_d": "The electronegativity of the oxygen atoms in oxides, peroxides and superoxides increases in the respective order.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is correct regarding water solubility of some compounds?", "option_a": "PH3 has higher water solubility than NH3.", "option_b": "Water solubility of gases increases with the increase of temperature.", "option_c": "SO2 is highly water soluble than CO2.", "option_d": "Solubility of most of the ionic compounds decrease with the increase of temperature.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement is correct regarding group 16 elements?", "option_a": "H2O2 is a planer, polar molecule.", "option_b": "Rhombic sulfur is stable at 100C.", "option_c": "Sulfurous acid solution contains few amount of sulfuric acid.", "option_d": "O2 is a stronger oxidizing agent than O3.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement is correct regarding gases He, N2 and CO2?", "option_a": "At the considered temperature under high pressure, molar volumes of them decreases in the order of He, N2, CO2.", "option_b": "Maximum temperature required to liquefy these gas molecules increases in the order of He, N2,CO2.", "option_c": "At a certain temperature, average kinetic energy of these gas molecules increases in the order of He, N2, CO2.", "option_d": "Under normal conditions, the pressure decrease due to attraction among gas molecules decreases in the order of He, N2, CO2.", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding the oxy acids of Nitrogen HNO3, HNO2 is/are true? (a) Photo dissociation of HNO3 is a disproportionation reaction. (b) Electronegativity of N atom in HNO2 is greater than that of HNO3 (c) HNO3 and NO are formed by the disproportionation reaction of HNO2 (d) Non-metallic elements such as Carbon and Sulphur are oxidized to their highest oxidation states by Con.HNO3", "option_a": "a,b,d", "option_b": "a,d", "option_c": "all are correct", "option_d": "a only", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following is incorrect regarding Nitrogen and its compounds?", "option_a": "N is a non-metallic element able to have all the oxidation numbers from – 3 to +5", "option_b": "Its aqueous chloride solution possess bleaching ability as well as disinfectant property.", "option_c": "Thermal decomposition reaction of NH4NO3 is a disproportionation reaction in which neutral products are formed.", "option_d": "con.HNO3 could oxidize C, S and P to their highest oxidation states.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is incorrect?", "option_a": "Water is an amphiprotic compound.", "option_b": "Metallic radius of Al is greater than that of covalent radius.", "option_c": "A colourless and odourless gas is evolved when Na2CO3 solid is added to the AlCl solution.", "option_d": "Bond energy of F2 is greater than that of Cl2", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "A)C6H5OH B)CH3CH2OH C)CH3COOH D)CH3OH E)H2O Most probable increasing order of electronegativity of O atom in O – H bonds in each of the above compounds is,", "option_a": "B < D < E < A < C", "option_b": "D < B < E < A < C", "option_c": "B < D < A < E < C", "option_d": "E < D < B < A < C", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which one of the following statements is incorrect regarding the chemistry of (Na) sodium and its compounds", "option_a": "Na reacts with oxygen to produce NaHO and NaHOH only.", "option_b": "All the compounds of Na are easily soluble in water.", "option_c": "In flame test, salts of sodium produce red – purple colour.", "option_d": "NaHCOH is a carbonate of basic metal and not decomposed by heat", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Select the incorrect statement from the following", "option_a": "𝐾𝐹 Shows the greatest ionic among 𝐿𝑖𝐹,𝐾𝐹 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐾𝐼", "option_b": "Among group 2 carbonates 𝐵𝑎𝐶𝑂3 has the highest thermal stability", "option_c": "𝐶𝑟 atom has the highest number of unpaired electron in its ground state.", "option_d": "𝑋𝑒 has the highest boiling point among noble gas", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement regarding 3d elements is incorrect?", "option_a": "Ti, V and Cr have high melting points.", "option_b": "Cr shows oxidation states from +2 to +6 in its compounds.", "option_c": "Sc has the highest reducing ability among all the 3d elements.", "option_d": "All of them use 3d electrons along with 4s electrons in compound formation.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct? (a) Na3N is not produced in the reaction between Na and N2. (b) The third ionization energy of Na is much greater than the first ionization energy of it. (c) The solubility of all salts of alkali earth metals decreases along the group from top to bottom.", "option_a": "a,b", "option_b": "b,c", "option_c": "a,b,c", "option_d": "a only", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "easy"}]

instruction:
You are an A-Level Chemistry examiner.
Topic: A-Level inorganic chemistry practice paper. Block: mixed_inorganic. Student level: mixed.

Notes:
Use A-Level inorganic chemistry knowledge and the exam style from the verified question bank. The questions may cover s-block, p-block, d-block, periodic trends, oxides, hydrides, acids, transition metal chemistry, qualitative analysis, solubility, thermal stability, redox behaviour, and molecular structure. Keep the output format strict and consistent.

Generate exactly 15 multiple-choice A-Level chemistry questions as a JSON array.
The 15 questions must include exactly 5 easy, 5 medium, and 5 hard questions, in any order.
Each object must have exactly these keys: question_text, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_answer (value: A, B, C, or D), difficulty (value: easy, medium, or hard).
Output raw JSON array only. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
output: [{"question_text": "Which of the following set of quantum number is not possible for the valence electron of the most stableion of Sc?", "option_a": "(3,2,-1,+ ½ )", "option_b": "(3,0, 0, + ½ )", "option_c": "(3,1,0, - ½ )", "option_d": "(3,1,-1, + ½ )", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding 3d transition elements is true?", "option_a": "The oxy anions of Cr, CrO₄²⁻ and Cr₂O₇²⁻are stable in acidic medium and basic mediumrespectively.", "option_b": "The oxide MnO₂ of Mn is amphoteric in nature and acts as a very good oxidizing agent in neutral medium.", "option_c": "4S orbital is found as empty in all the stable positive ions of these elements.", "option_d": "Only Ti and Mn can form dioxides among these elements.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following relationships is true regarding the bond angles of following molecules / ions", "option_a": "NO₃⁻ > NO₄³⁻", "option_b": "PH₃ > NH₃", "option_c": "BF₄⁻ > NH₄⁺", "option_d": "ClO₃⁻ > ClO₄⁻", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Select the correct statement about 3𝑑 element.", "option_a": "All 3𝑑 elements are called Transition element.", "option_b": "Among 3𝑑 elements 𝐶𝑢 has highest - Pauling electronegativity value.", "option_c": "Among 3𝑑 elements 𝑉 (vanadium) has lowest first ionization energy.", "option_d": "Among 3𝑑 elements 𝐶𝑟 has highest - melting point in Celsius.", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "The incorrect statement regarding H2O2 is", "option_a": "It is a light blue coloured viscous liquid", "option_b": "The dissociation of it in the presence of light is a disproportionation reaction", "option_c": "It gives O2 as a product when it acts as a reducing agent.", "option_d": "When reacting with PbS solid, it gives PbO and a pale yellow coloured sulphur residue", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which one of the following statements is not true regarding 𝐶𝑟𝑂₄²⁻ 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐶𝑟₂𝑂₇²⁻", "option_a": "Both contain 𝐶𝑟 in its highest oxidation states.", "option_b": "Both oxidized 𝐼⁻ to 𝐼₂.", "option_c": "Both are good oxidizing agent.", "option_d": "Both are tetrahedral shapes.", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding carbon and its compounds is/are correct? (a) Solid CO2 is used in food industry and producing artificial rain. (b) Among the allotropes of carbon, diamond has sp3 hybridized carbon atoms while graphite and fullerene have sp2 hybridized carbon atoms. (c) Acidity of oxyacids H2C2O4, H2CO3 and HCOOH varies as H2C2O4 < HCOOH < H2CO3 (d) Metal carbides such as Be2C, Mg2C3 and CaC2 produces CH4, C3H4 and C2H2 gases respectively in reaction with water.", "option_a": "a,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "all are correct", "option_d": "a,b,d", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Among 𝐴𝑙2𝑂3,𝑆𝑖𝑂2,𝑃2𝑂3 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑆𝑂2 the correct order of acidic strength is", "option_a": "𝐴𝑙2𝑂3 < 𝑆𝑖𝑂2 < 𝑃2𝑂3 < 𝑆𝑂2", "option_b": "𝐴𝑙2𝑂3 < 𝑆𝑖𝑂2 < 𝑆𝑂2 <𝑃2𝑂3", "option_c": "𝑆𝑖𝑂2 < 𝐴𝑙2𝑂3 < 𝑃2𝑂3 < 𝑆𝑂2", "option_d": "𝑆𝑖𝑂2 < 𝐴𝑙2𝑂3 < 𝑆𝑂2 < 𝑃2𝑂3", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding 3d – transition elements and their compounds is false?", "option_a": "The Highest valency of the elements from Ti to Cr is equal to number of unpaired electrons in their ground state.", "option_b": "Ti,Cr,V and Mn can form dioxides.", "option_c": "Bivalent metal hydroxides of Mn,Fe and Co can be oxidized easily in the atmosphere.", "option_d": "V,Cr and Mn can form acidic,amphoteric and basic oxides.", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct? (a) The bond angle of NH3 molecule is greater than that of NF3 molecule (b) NF3 molecule is more polar than NH3 molecule (c) Ag. 2CO3 is easily soluble in diluted HNO3 solution, while AgCl is insoluble.", "option_a": "a,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "all are correct", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding the chemistry of Sulphur (S) is false?", "option_a": "An aqueous solution of SCl4 shows bleaching property.", "option_b": "S8 molecular units are present in both allotropic forms of sulphur such as rhombic sulphur and monoclinic sulphur.", "option_c": "When sulphur is oxidized by hot concentrated nitric acid, the final product is limited to SO2 in which the oxidation state of S is +4 .", "option_d": "Oxidizing ability of peroxy sulphuric acid (H2SO5) is greater than that of sulphuric acid.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement is incorrect regarding H2S?", "option_a": "H2S can act as both oxidizing agent and reducing agent.", "option_b": "Bond angle decreases in the order of H2Se, H2S and H2O", "option_c": "Conjugate base of H2S is HS͞", "option_d": "When H2S is passed through acidified Pb(NO3)2(aq), Cu(NO3)2(aq) and Hg(NO3)2(aq) solutions black precipitates are obtained.", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false with regard to Nitrogen and its compounds.", "option_a": "Nitrogen has Oxidation states in the range -3 to +5", "option_b": "𝑁𝑂2 react with 𝑑𝑖𝑙 𝑁𝑎𝑂𝐻 it can under go disproportionation reaction.", "option_c": "Never 𝐿𝑖 does not react with 𝑁2 gas.", "option_d": "In asymmetric 𝑁2 𝑂3 molecules oxidation states of two nitrogen are +2 and +4.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding nitrogen (N) is false ?", "option_a": "The maximum number of electrons which can be present in the valent shell of nitrogen in the compounds of nitrogen is 8.", "option_b": "Only four orbitals are present in the valent shell of nitrogen.", "option_c": "Nitrogen shows the highest valency in second period elements.", "option_d": "According to Pauling’s scale electronegativity of nitrogen is greater than that of chlorine.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "3d transition metals L, M and N react with Cl2 and each gives yellow coloured anhydrous chloride solid separately. When water is added to the chloride compounds of L, M and N separately they give blue,yellow and green colour solutions respectively. The metals L, M and N respectively are", "option_a": "Ni, Co, Fe", "option_b": "Ni, Mn, Cr", "option_c": "Ni, Cu, Fe", "option_d": "Cu, Fe, Ni", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}]

instruction:
You are an A-Level Chemistry examiner.
Topic: A-Level inorganic chemistry — s, p and d block. Block: mixed_inorganic. Student level: mixed.

Notes:
Use A-Level inorganic chemistry knowledge and the exam style from the verified question bank. The questions may cover s-block, p-block, d-block, periodic trends, oxides, hydrides, acids, transition metal chemistry, qualitative analysis, solubility, thermal stability, redox behaviour, and molecular structure. Keep the output format strict and consistent.

Generate exactly 15 multiple-choice A-Level chemistry questions as a JSON array.
The 15 questions must include exactly 5 easy, 5 medium, and 5 hard questions, in any order.
Each object must have exactly these keys: question_text, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_answer (value: A, B, C, or D), difficulty (value: easy, medium, or hard).
Output raw JSON array only. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
output: [{"question_text": "When the compounds given below are heated, they decompose according to the reaction, MCO3(s)-----> MO(s) + CO2(g) Identify the compound with the lowest decomposition temperature.", "option_a": "BeCO3", "option_b": "MgCO3", "option_c": "CaCO3", "option_d": "SrCO3", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "The colours of AgBr(s) and Agl(s) are pale yellow and yellow, respectively. A solution of Nal(aq) was added to a test tube containing AgBr(s). When the contents in the test tube were stirred, its colour turned yellow. The following ideas were proposed to explain this observation. I. The reaction, AgBr(s) + I⁻(aq) -> Agl(s) + Br⁻(aq) occurs. II. Kₛₚ of AgBr(s) > Kₛₚ of AgI(s) III. Kₛₚ of AgBr(s) < Kₛₚ of Agl(s) IV. Concentration of I⁻(aq) is decreased. V. Concentration of Br⁻(aq) is decreased. Which of the above are correct about this observation?", "option_a": "I, III and V", "option_b": "I, IV and V", "option_c": "I, II and IV", "option_d": "I, II and V", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) The colours of the aqueous solutions of 𝑀𝑛𝑂4− 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐶𝑟𝑂42− ions are due to the electron transitions among degenerate 𝑑 orbitals. (b) When 𝐾3[𝐹𝑒(𝐶𝑁)6] is added to a 𝐹𝑒2+ solution blue precipitate of 𝐹𝑒4[𝐹𝑒(𝐶𝑁)6] is obtained. (c) Although the electronegativity of the 3𝑑 elements in general increases from 𝑆𝑐 to 𝑍𝑛, it shows decrease in 𝑀𝑛 and 𝑍𝑛. (d) Among 3𝑑 elements, the elements having the highest and the lowest melting points are 𝑉 and 𝑍𝑛 respectively", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b,c,d", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding group 17 elements and some of their compounds is correct?", "option_a": "Bond dissociation energies of them follow the order F₂ > Cl₂ > Br₂ > I₂", "option_b": "Boiling points of their hydrides increase in the order HF < HCl < HBr < HI", "option_c": "Order of the oxidizing ability of the oxyacids of 𝐶𝑙 is HOCl > HClO₂ > HClO₃ > HClO₄", "option_d": "Compounds of 𝐵𝑟 with oxidation number +7 are stable at room temperature.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding s-block elements? (a) The metallic radii of Group II elements are less than the metallic radii of the corresponding Group I elements. (b) The first ionization energies of Group II elements are greater than the first ionization energies of the corresponding Group I elements. (c) The densities of Group II elements are lower than the densities of Group I elements. (d) Group II elements have weaker metallic bonds than Group I elements.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b,c,d", "option_d": "a,b", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding hydrogen halides? (a) Hydrogen halides are acidic in water. (b) The bond dissociation energies of hydrogen halides decrease when going down the group. (c) The acidic strength of hydrogen halides decreases when going down the group. (d) The bond length of hydrogen halides decreases when going down the group.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements regarding some complex ions formed by 3d elements is false?", "option_a": "Ni²⁺ and Cu²⁺ aqueous solution form deep blue solutions with excess NH₃(aq)", "option_b": "Aqueous solutions of Cu²⁺,Ni²⁺ 𝑎𝑛𝑑 Fe³⁺ produce yellow coloured complex ions with concentrated 𝐻𝐶𝑙.", "option_c": "Mn²+and Co²+ ions form blue coloured complexes with concentrated HCl", "option_d": "Fe³⁺, Cr³⁺ and Mn²⁺ form insoluble hydroxides with excess NH₃(aq)", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true with regard to p-block elements and their compounds? (a) The reaction of PCl5 and SCl2 with water gives H3PO (aq) and S(s) as one of the products respectively. (b) Reaction of Cl2(g) with water and decomposition of H2O2(aq) are examples of disproportionation reactions. (c) A product obtained in the reaction of Cl2(g) with excess NH3(g) can be used to disinfect water. (d) SO2(g) cannot act as a reducing agent.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "The correct statement/statements related to group 15 elements and some of their compounds is/are, (a) 𝑁𝐶𝑙3 can be used to disinfect water. (b) The elements in this group which can form cations are precipitated in the same group in the qualitative analysis tests. (c) All the chlorides of the elements in this group undergo hydrolysis to give only acidic solutions. (d) The dipole moment of 𝑁𝐹3 is greater than the dipole moment of 𝑁𝐻3", "option_a": "a,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b,c,d", "option_d": "a,b", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is /are false with regard to 𝐻2𝑂2? a) The oxygen atoms in 𝐻2𝑂2 are 𝑆𝑃 hybridized. b) 𝐻2𝑂2 is a planer molecule. c) In gaseous state 𝐻2𝑂2 has bent molecular shape. d) The bond angle in the gaseous phase for 𝐻−𝑂−𝑂 is 94.8.", "option_a": "a,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b,c,d", "option_d": "a,b", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "The incorrect statement regarding sulfur and some of its compounds,", "option_a": "The reaction of sulfur with 𝑁𝑎𝑂𝐻(𝑎𝑞) is a disproportination.", "option_b": "Plastic sulfur and colloidal sulfur are two non-crystalline allotropes of sulfur.", "option_c": "Since thiosulpuric acid is unstable at room temperature it decomposes as 𝑆, 𝑆𝑂2 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐻2𝑂", "option_d": "At temperature less than 95℃ , rhombic sulfur converts to monoclinic sulfur.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is incorrect regarding d-block elements?", "option_a": "VO is a basic oxide while V₂O₅ is an acidic oxide.", "option_b": "In Mn₃O₄, manganese is present as a mixture of Mn(II) and Mn(III).", "option_c": "When NH₄CI/NH OH are added to an aqueous solution containing Fe³⁺ and Co²⁺, only Fe³⁺ precipitates.", "option_d": "The white precipitate formed on addition of dil. NaOH to ZnCl₂(aq) is insoluble in dil. HCI.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Identify the incorrect statement with regard to the thermal decomposition of the salts given below. NH4CI, NH4NO2,NH4NO3,(NH4)2CO3 and (NH4)2Cr2O7", "option_a": "Only two of the salts give NH3 as a product.", "option_b": "Only two of the salts give N2 as a product.", "option_c": "Only two of the salts give an acidic gas as a product.", "option_d": "Only two of the salts give H2O as a product.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "The correct statement/ statements regarding 𝐻2𝑂2 is/are, (a) 𝐻2𝑂2 can act as an oxidizing agent and a reducing agent. (b) 𝐻2𝑂2 is a polar and planar molecule. (c) Under standard conditions, boiling point of 𝐻2𝑂2 is less than the boiling point of 𝐻2𝑂. (d) 𝑆𝑂2 is oxidized to 𝐻2𝑆𝑂4 in the reaction of 𝑆𝑂2 with 𝐻2𝑂2.", "option_a": "a,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b,c,d", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which one of the following statements is true with regard to Al and some of its compounds?", "option_a": "Al is the most abundant element found in the earth crust.", "option_b": "When AlCl3 forms the stable state in gaseous state, the 𝐶𝑙 atom in it acts as a Lewis acid.", "option_c": "Al(OH)3 is formed due to the hydrolysis of AlCl3", "option_d": "CO2 gas is evolved when an aqueous solution of Al(NO3)3 is treated with Na2CO3(aq)", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}]

instruction:
You are an A-Level Chemistry examiner.
Topic: A-Level inorganic chemistry — periodic trends and reactions. Block: mixed_inorganic. Student level: mixed.

Notes:
Use A-Level inorganic chemistry knowledge and the exam style from the verified question bank. The questions may cover s-block, p-block, d-block, periodic trends, oxides, hydrides, acids, transition metal chemistry, qualitative analysis, solubility, thermal stability, redox behaviour, and molecular structure. Keep the output format strict and consistent.

Generate exactly 15 multiple-choice A-Level chemistry questions as a JSON array.
The 15 questions must include exactly 5 easy, 5 medium, and 5 hard questions, in any order.
Each object must have exactly these keys: question_text, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_answer (value: A, B, C, or D), difficulty (value: easy, medium, or hard).
Output raw JSON array only. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
output: [{"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) When H2S gas reacts with an acidic solution ofK2Cr2O7, elemental sulphur is formed. (b) H2S gas can act as a reducing agent in acidicmedium (c) All three ions Ni²⁺(aq), Cu²⁺(aq) and Zn²⁺(aq) give ammine complexes when individually treated with excess NH4OH(aq).", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "a only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true with regard to s and p block elements and their compounds? (a) Be reacts with hydrogen gas to produce an ionic metal hydride. (b) Mg has the highest electronegativity among s-block elements. (c) NH3, SO2 and H2S can act as oxidizing agents as well as reducing agents. (d) Na and Ba react with excess oxygen gas when heated to give Na2O2 and BaO2 respectively.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) Metallic bonding in Group 1 elements (Li-Cs) is weaker than metallic bonding in Group 2 elements (Be-Ba). (b) Metallic bonding involves only one valence electron in Group 1 elements whereas two electrons are involved in Group 2 elements. (C) Aqueous solutions of Ni2+, Cu2t and Zn2t when individually treated with excess NH OH(aq) do not give permanent precipitates.", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "a only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Identify the correct statement from the following.", "option_a": "The bond angle of NF3 is larger than the bond angle of NH3.", "option_b": "Elements in group 17 (or 7A) exhibit oxidation states from -1 to +7.", "option_c": "Monoclinic sulphur is the most stable allotrope of sulphur at room temperature.", "option_d": "Aluminium chloride satisfies the octet rule in the gaseous state.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct with regard to the two allotropes of carbon, namely, graphite and diamond? (a) Carbon atoms in diamond are tetrahedrally surrounded by four other carbon atoms to give a three-dimensional lattice. (b) Because graphite is composed of two-dimensional layers held together by weak van der Waals forces (secondary interactions), it acts as a good lubricant. (c) Diamond is a good conductor of heat and electricity. (d) Graphite has a considerably higher melting point than diamond.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "b,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct with regard to nitric acid (HNO3) and its salts? (a) Both dilute and concentrated HNO3 act as oxidizing agents. (b) Thermal decomposition of NH4NO3 gives N₂O and water. (c) N-O bonds of HNO3, are all equal in length. (d) Carbon does not react with concentrated HNO3 even when heated.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "b,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct with regard to the compounds/ions formed by d block elements that belong to the fourth period? (a) Cr2O3 is expected to react with strong acids and strong bases. (b) When NaOH(aq) is added to solutions containing Fe²⁺(aq), Fe³⁺(aq), Mn²⁺(aq) and N²⁺(aq) precipitates which are insoluble in excess NaOH(aq) are formed. (c) Both KMnO4, and K2Cr2O7, are strong oxidizing agents capable of converting H2O2 to O₂ gas under acidic conditions. (d) The IUPAC name of [CuCl4]²⁻ is tetrachlorocuprate(II) ion.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,d", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement/s is/are correct with regard to 3d-block elements and their compounds? (a) Among the 3d-block elements, Sc is not considered as a transition element. (b) The radii of atoms (Sc to Cu) decrease from left to right. (c) [Ni(NH₃)₆]²⁺ is blue in colour whereas [Zn(NH₃)₄]²⁺ is colourless. (d) The IUPAC name of K2NiCl4 is dipotassium tetrachloronickelate(II)", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) N2(g) cannot behave as an oxidizing agent. (b) When heated N2(g) reacts with Li to give an ionic product which reacts with water liberating NH2(g) (c) The electronic configuration of Mn is more stable than the electronic configurations of Cr and Fe.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) Among the oxides of Cr and Mn, CrO and MnO are acidic, while CrO3 and Mn2O7 are basic. (b) The acidic/basic nature of the oxides of Cr and Mn is dependant on the oxidation number of the metal. (c) HOCl has the highest oxidizing ability among the oxoacids of chlorine", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "a only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct with regard to colours observed for precipitates/solutions when (i) excess NaOH(aq) and (ii) excess NH4OH(aq) are added separately to aqueous solutions of each of the following cations Co2+, Ni2+, Cu2t and Zn2+? (a) Co²⁺ gives (i) a brown precipitate and (ii) a red solution respectively. (b) Ni²⁺ gives (i) a blue precipitate and (ii) a green solution respectively. (c) Cu²⁺ gives (i) a blue precipitate and (ii) a dark blue solution respectively. (d) Zn²⁺ gives (i) a colourless solution and (ii) a colourless solution respectively.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) H2S(g) can act as a reducing agent as well as an oxidizing agent under suitable conditions. (b) Sulphur is a non metal with oxidation numbers in the range -2 to +6. (c) The electronegativity of Mn is lower than the electronegativity of Cr and Fe.", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) When an acidic solution of MnO4 is treated with H2O2, it turns colourless with the evolution of O2, whereas, an acidic solution of Fe²⁺ on treatment with H2O2 turns yellow-brown. (b) H2O2 can act as an oxidizing agent as well as a reducing agent in acidic medium. (c) Cl2 gas undergoes disproportionation on reaction with water giving HOCl(aq) and HCl(aq).", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "a only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding 3d-block elements and their compounds (a) The correct IUPAC name of [Cr(NH3)6]Br, is hexaamminechromium(III) tribromide. (b) Based on the electronic configurations of the 3d-block metals, Zn is expected to have the lowest melting point. (c) Cu shows the lowest stable oxidation state among 3d-block elements. (d) CrO3 dissolves in aqueous NaOH and gives Cr2O, ion.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is incorrect with regard to element Lithium (Li) and its compounds?", "option_a": "Among the Group I elements from Li - Cs, lithium has the most negative value for electron gain energy.", "option_b": "Lithium forms two products when heated in air.", "option_c": "Considering the gases evolved, upon heating LiNO3(s) produces two gases whereas Li2CO3(s) gives only one gas.", "option_d": "Among Group I elements, lithium has the weakest metallic bonding.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}]

instruction:
You are an A-Level Chemistry examiner.
Topic: A-Level inorganic chemistry — transition metals and qualitative analysis. Block: mixed_inorganic. Student level: mixed.

Notes:
Use A-Level inorganic chemistry knowledge and the exam style from the verified question bank. The questions may cover s-block, p-block, d-block, periodic trends, oxides, hydrides, acids, transition metal chemistry, qualitative analysis, solubility, thermal stability, redox behaviour, and molecular structure. Keep the output format strict and consistent.

Generate exactly 15 multiple-choice A-Level chemistry questions as a JSON array.
The 15 questions must include exactly 5 easy, 5 medium, and 5 hard questions, in any order.
Each object must have exactly these keys: question_text, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_answer (value: A, B, C, or D), difficulty (value: easy, medium, or hard).
Output raw JSON array only. No markdown, no explanation, no extra text.
output: [{"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) BaCO3 is more thermally stable than MgCO3. (b) Polarizing power of group two cations decreases down the group. (c) Nitrogen is less electronegative than oxygen.", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "a only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement/s is/are true with regard to halogens? (a) The boiling points of halogens increase down the group. (b) Unlike other halogens, fluorine always has an oxidation state of (-1) except in F2. (c) All halogens are good reducing agents. (d) Although fluorine is the most reactive of all the elements in the Periodic Table, it does not react with inert gases.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false regarding 3d transition metals and their compounds?", "option_a": "Oxides of some metals are amphoteric.", "option_b": "Some metals and metal oxides are used in industry as catalysts.", "option_c": "Elcctronegativity of 3d transition metals is higher than 4s metals.", "option_d": "Oxoions such as MnO₄⁻ Cr₂O₇²⁻ are resistant to reduction.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement/statements is/are correct with regard to simple covalent molecules containing oxygen and sulphur atoms? (a) H2O shows amphoteric properties. (b) The boiling point of H2O2 is higher than the boiling point of H2O. (c) H2O2 can act as an oxidizing agent only in an acidic medium. (d) Both H2S and SO2 have the capacity to act only as reducing agents.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "The incorrect statement with regard to the oxoacids of chlorine HOCl, HClO2, HClO3 and HClO4 is,", "option_a": "The shapes around chlorine in HClO2, HClO3 and HClO4 respectively are angular, pyramidal and tetrahedral.", "option_b": "The oxidation states of chlorine in HOCl, HClO2, HClO3 and HClO4 respectively are +1,+3, +5 and +7.", "option_c": "The acid strength of the oxoacids varies as HOCl < HClO2 < HClO3 < HClO4", "option_d": "All these oxoacids contain at least one double bond.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "When (NH4)2CO3(s), (NH2)2Cr2O7,(s) and NH4NO3(s) are heated, the nitrogen containing compounds obtained are respectively,", "option_a": "NH3, N2 and NO2", "option_b": "N2O, N2 and NH3", "option_c": "NH3,N2 and N2O", "option_d": "N2, N2O and NH3", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false with regard to the chemistry of Lithium (Li) and its compounds?", "option_a": "Lithium reacts with oxygen gas to give Li2O.", "option_b": "Lithium has the highest melting point among the group 1 metals.", "option_c": "The basicity of LiOH is less than that of NaOH.", "option_d": "LiCl gives a blue colour when subjected to the flame test.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false with regard to the s-block elements in the Periodic Table?", "option_a": "All elements in group I react with water liberating H2 gas.", "option_b": "All elements in group I except Li react with N2 gas.", "option_c": "All elements in group II react with N2 gas.", "option_d": "Na reacts with excess O2 to give Na2O2 whereas K gives KO2.", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false?", "option_a": "Although the carbonates of all the group two metals in the Periodic Table are insoluble in water, their bicarbonates are soluble.", "option_b": "The hydroxides of all the group two metals in the Periodic Table are soluble in water.", "option_c": "The nitrates of all the group two metals in the Periodic Table are soluble in water.", "option_d": "The oxides and hydroxides of Na and Mg show basic properties whereas the oxide and hydroxide of Al show amphoteric properties.", "correct_answer": "B", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is/are true? (a) Sucrose when treated with concentrated H2SO4 gives a black mass. (b) Concentrated H2SO4 is a strong oxidizing agent. (c) The solubility of barium oxalate, BaC2O4(s) is less in acidic aqueous medium than in water.", "option_a": "c only", "option_b": "a only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false with regard to NH3?", "option_a": "NH3 can act only as a base.", "option_b": "NH3 burns in oxygen to give N2 gas.", "option_c": "NH3 gives a brown colour with Nessler's reagent.", "option_d": "NH3 reacts with Li to give Li3N and H2 gas.", "correct_answer": "A", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statement/statements is/are correct with regard to 3d-block elements? (a) Zn has the highest first ionization energy among the 3d-block elements. (b) In contrast to the ions of most main group elements (s and p-block), 3d-block metal ions rarely attain the noble gas configuration. (c) Although the electronegativities of 3d-block elements are higher than the electronegativities of the corresponding s-block elements, their atomic radii are smaller than the atomic radii of the corresponding s-block elements. (d) The 3d-block elements that form colourless compounds are Ti and Zn.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,b,c", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "easy"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is false with regard to 3d-elements of the Periodic Table?", "option_a": "Atomic radii are smaller than the atomic radii of the s-block elements in the same period.", "option_b": "Densities are higher than the densities of the s-block elements in the same period.", "option_c": "V2O5. CrO3 and Mn2O7 are acidic oxides.", "option_d": "First ionization energies are less than the first ionization energies of the s-block elements in the same period.", "correct_answer": "D", "difficulty": "medium"}, {"question_text": "Which of the fllowing statements is/are false with regard to nitric acid? (a) Pure nitric acid is a light yellow liquid. (b) All N-O bond lengths in nitric acid are equal. (c) Nitric acid cannot act as a reducing agent. (d) It is used in the manufacture of an important fertilizer, ammonium nitrate.", "option_a": "c,d", "option_b": "d only", "option_c": "a,b", "option_d": "a,c", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}, {"question_text": "Which of the following statements is true with regard to transition metals and their compounds?", "option_a": "The electronic configuration of copper is 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 3d¹⁰.", "option_b": "All elements that have d-electrons are 'transition elements'.", "option_c": "The electronic configuration of Ti in TiO2 is the same as that of Sc in ScCl3.", "option_d": "Acidity of the oxides of a given transition metal decreases with increase in oxidation state of the metal ion.", "correct_answer": "C", "difficulty": "hard"}]
